projectSummary: This project seeks to address health needs stemming from displacement due to conflict and floods, as well as disease outbreaks - including cholera, in six designated counties of South Sudan: Canal Pigi, Fangak, Ayod, Akobo, Panyikang and Fashoda. The project targets a total of 154,000 affected people, including 36,824 women, 38,328 men, 78,848 children, and including 23,100 persons with disabilities.
projectTitle: Life-saving humanitarian response for crisis-affected populations in Canal Pigi, Fangak, Ayod, Akobo, Panyikang and Fashoda counties in South Sudan.

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to improve access to lifesaving sexual and reproductive health services for 40,675 women and girls of reproductive age in six high-priority counties of Upper Nile and Jonglei States. This UNFPA project provides case management for survivors of gender-based violence and protection support, including cash assistance, to 1,000 survivors in the targeted counties. Additionally, the project aims to enhance dignity and reduce health risks for women and girls through the distribution of 84 reproductive health kits and 5,455 dignity kits. The project provides life-saving assistance to 47,837 people, including 23,919 women, 7,655 men, 16,263 children, and including 3,349 persons with disabilities.
projectTitle: Improving Access to Emergency SRH and GBV Prevention and Response for Vulnerable Women and Girls in Upper Nile and Jonglei States

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to provide life-saving food and nutrition assistance to 103,158 vulnerable and flood-affected populations in six high-priority counties of South Sudan (Canal/Pigi, Fangak, Ayod, Akobo, Panyikang, and Fashoda) through targeted general food distribution, supplementary feeding programs, and early flood mitigation interventions, thereby reducing acute food insecurity and preventing further deterioration in nutritional status. This WFP project provides a range of activities. The project provides life-saving assistance to 103,158 people, including 23,264 women, 10,482 men, and 69,412 children.
projectTitle: Emergency food and nutrition assistance to the most vulnerable and food insecure people affected by conflict and flooding

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to reduce the adverse impact of flooding on the lives and livelihoods of 65,000 vulnerable households in South Sudan, particularly those at heightened risk due to escalating conflict, anticipated floods, food insecurity, and the ongoing cholera outbreak. This FAO project provides interventions focus on the most affected counties, namely Canal/Pigi, Fangak, Akobo, Ayod, Panyikang, and Fashoda. The project provides life-saving assistance to 390,000 people, including 195,000 women, 117,000 men, and 78,000 children.
projectTitle: Life-saving emergency livelihood response in South Sudan

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to target 125,043 people in Fangak, Ayod, Akobo, Canal/Pigi, Panyikang, and Fashoda, affected by conflict, food insecurity, cholera, and anticipated flooding. It aims to deliver integrated WASH and Nutrition services, including early detection and treatment of wasting, and preventive support. Communities will be empowered with knowledge and tools to practice safe hygiene and access services, improving health and resilience. The project delivers life-saving assistance to 175,080 people, including 37,904 women, 29,376 men, 107,800 children, and including 26,258 persons with disabilities.
projectTitle: Integrated WASH and Nutrition Response in Fangak, Ayod, Akobo, Canal Pigi, Panyikang, Fashoda affected by multiple crisis: conflict, food insecurity, cholera and anticipatory action against flooding.

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to collect data to inform protection advocacy and emergency response (assessment, protection monitoring, enrollment, and profiling), provide life-saving protection support, including cash assistance to conflict and flood-affected people, and alleviate the suffering of conflict and flood-affected populations by restoring their shelters and improving their living conditions, thereby enhancing their resilience to ongoing climate challenges. This UNHCR project provides these activities among others. The project provides life-saving assistance to 72,747 people, including 19,153 women, 18,586 men, 35,008 children, and including 11,732 persons with disabilities.
projectTitle: Provision of lifesaving assistance to conflict and flood affected persons in South Sudan
agencyName: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees
implementingAgencyName: United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees

projectSummary: For the implementation of this project, WFP delivers a rapid, context appropriate emergency food and nutrition response across six counties, Nyirol, Uror, Ulang, Duk, Akobo, and Awerial prioritizing households in IPC Phase 4, and IDPs. WFP applies a complementary funding model, using CERF resources to procure pulses and vegetable oil, while cereals will be sourced from other donor contributions to ensure affected people receive a full, nutritionally complete food basket. The project targets a total of 31,321 affected people with life-saving Food Security and Nutrition activities.
projectTitle: Emergency Food and Nutrition Assistance to Conflict Affected Populations in Jonglei, Upper Nile and Lakes State

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to contribute to reducing morbidity and mortality risks, malnutrition, and exposure to protection risks among conflict-affected internally displaced persons in Jonglei, Upper Nile, and Lakes States, including the priority counties of Duk, Uror, Nyirol, Akobo, Awerial, and Ulang. This UNICEF project provides WASH, Nutrition, and Child Protection services. The project provides life-saving assistance to 173,386 people, including 47,328 women, 28,235 men, 97,823 children, and including 20,435 people with disabilities.
projectTitle: Life-Saving Integrated WASH, Nutrition and Child Protection Response for Conflict-Affected Internally Displaced Persons in Jonglei, Upper Nile and Lakes States, South Sudan.

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to strengthen emergency health services and disease prevention in Nyirol, Uror, Akobo, Duk, Awerial, and Ulang by deploying technical health experts, supporting static and mobile health centers, enhancing outbreak preparedness, and training health workers to deliver quality care to 53,336 vulnerable people affected by conflict, climate shocks, and disease outbreaks. This WHO project provides deployment of technical health experts, support to static and mobile health centers, enhancement of outbreak preparedness, and training of health workers to deliver quality care. The project provides life-saving assistance to 53,335 people, including 16,133 women, 15,603 men, 21,599 children, and including 8,001 people with disabilities.
projectTitle: Strengthening Emergency Health Services and Disease Prevention in Nyirol, Uror, Akobo, Duk, Awerial, and Ulang

projectSummary: This project supports the provision of an integrated, life-saving Sexual and Reproductive Health (SRH) and Gender-Based Violence (GBV) response for conflict-displaced women and girls in Jonglei, Upper Nile and Lakes states, where active conflict, inter-communal violence and large-scale displacement have severely disrupted access to essential services. The project provides life-saving assistance to 32,664 people, including 16,332 women, 5,226 men, 11,106 children, and including 2,939 people with disabilities.
projectTitle: Integrated Lifesaving Sexual and Reproductive Health and GBV Response for Conflict-Displaced Women and Girls in Jonglei, Upper Nile and Lakes

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict on affected households by ensuring timely and adequate access to life-saving Shelter and Non-Food Item assistance through both in-kind and cash-based modalities, while strengthening their capacities to mitigate further harm and other risks. This IOM project provides assistance to internally displaced persons in Nyirol, Uror, Akobo, Duk, Awerial, and Ulang counties of Jonglei State, as well as internally displaced persons who have fled to Bor, South Sudan. The project provides life-saving assistance to 44,118 people, including 10,919 women, 8,934 men, 24,265 children, and including 6,618 people with disabilities.
projectTitle: Provision of Shelter and Non-Food Items Assistance to Vulnerable IDPs and Affected Host Community in Jonglei State, South Sudan

projectSummary: The main objective of this project is to provide safe and efficient air transport services to transport humanitarian personnel and urgently needed cargo to remote and hard to reach areas where urgent assistance is needed. This WFP project ensures that the UN Humanitarian Air Service (UNHAS) can enable humanitarian personnel and cargo to reach crisis-affected populations with life-saving assistance. The project supports 24,000 humanitarian personnel from 200 organizations and facilitates the air transport of 300 mt of cargo.
projectTitle: Provision of Critical Humanitarian Air Access Across South Sudan

projectSummary: This project delivers immediate life-saving food assistance to conflict-affected and severely food-insecure people in Akobo County, Jonglei State, while enabling the wider inter-agency humanitarian response through common logistics services in some of South Sudan’s most access-constrained operational environments. It directly targets 92,811 most vulnerable conflict-affected people in Akobo (Women: 14,850, Girls: 35,268, Men: 12,065, Boys: 30,628) with emergency in-kind food assistance during the peak lean season to prevent further deterioration in food consumption and acute food insecurity outcomes. Under the common services component, WFP, through the Logistics Cluster, provides critical inter-agency logistics support to facilitate the movement of life-saving humanitarian relief items into hard-to-reach operational areas in Akobo, including support to the broader multisectoral CERF response.
projectTitle: Emergency food assistance and humanitarian logistics support for conflict-affected populations in Akobo and high-severity counties in South Sudan

projectSummary: This project uses CERF funding to scale up integrated assistance in Akobo County, Jonglei State, and Upper Nile State, complementing ongoing response in Uror, Nyirol, and Abiemnhom counties. The additional funding allows UNICEF and partners to expand life-saving Health, Nutrition, WASH, Child Protection and Gender-Based Violence programming. The response increases access to essential services and reduce preventable morbidity, mortality, and protection risks among vulnerable populations. With this project, UNICEF supports a total of 27,979 (16,500 women, 5,500 girls, 3,066 men, and 2,913 boys) most vulnerable conflict-affected displaced children (girls and boys), pregnant and breastfeeding women, and survivors of violence, with lifesaving activities.
projectTitle: CERF RR allocation for conflict-related displacement/food insecurity in South Sudan - Top up to CERF-SSD-26-RR-CEF-38960

projectSummary: This WHO project provides rapid, life-saving health, nutrition, water, sanitation and hygiene, and outbreak readiness support to approximately 790,505 (205,531 women, 205,531 girls, 189,719 men, and 189,721 boys) most vulnerable affected people in high-priority locations in Jonglei State, South Sudan, including Akobo. It focuses on life-saving, time-bound and measurable activities, including life-saving emergency health services, medical management of severe acute malnutrition with complications and outbreak prevention.
projectTitle: Strengthening Emergency Health, Nutrition, WASH, and Outbreak Preparedness in Priority Counties of South Sudan: Akobo, Nyirol, Uror, Akoka, Nasir/Luakpiny, Ulang, and Abiemnhom Administrative Area

projectSummary: The project delivers an integrated life-saving maternal, sexual and reproductive health and gender-based violence prevention and response engagement for conflict-affected and food-insecure populations in Akobo East, where recent conflict escalation, displacement, severe food insecurity, and disruption of essential services have significantly increased humanitarian needs among women and girls. The project targets a total of 88,164 affected people (44,082 women, 22,923 girls, 14,106 men, and 7,053 boys) with lifesaving activities.
projectTitle: Rapid Life-saving Maternal, Sexual and Reproductive Health and Gender-based Violence Prevention and Response Services for Conflict and Food-insecure Communities in Akobo

projectSummary: IOM delivers a lifesaving humanitarian response in South Sudan through two complementary components: frontline Shelter and Non-Food Item assistance (including cash-based support and a core pipeline emergency supply mechanism), as well as Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) activities. The response targets a total of 44,652 affected people (10,752 women, 12,359 girls, 10,021 men, and 11,520 boys) including vulnerable returnees, internally displaced persons, and host communities affected by conflict and displacement, ensuring timely access to emergency shelter materials, essential nonfood items (NFI), and critical WASH supplies.
projectTitle: Provision of Emergency Shelter, Non-Food Items, and WASH Assistance to Vulnerable IDPs and Conflict-Affected Host Communities
